steps.title: Identify the shape
steps.content: The points rise faster as $x$ increases, so the model should be an increasing curve, not a straight line. That eliminates choices A and B.

steps.title: Compare choices C and D
steps.content: Choice C is an increasing curve, but it sits above all $8$ points, so it is not centered on the data. Choice D is also an increasing curve, and the data points are split around it: some above, some below, and some touching.

steps.title: Graph-check choice D
steps.content: The best model should follow the trend and have points on both sides of the curve. Only choice D does that, so D is correct.
